task_completion.description: Check if the JStock application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Check if the Portfolio tab or portfolio management interface is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Portfolio' tab is selected or a portfolio management dialog is open in JStock.

task_completion.description: Check if the portfolio has been renamed to 'Tech Growth Fund'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The portfolio name 'Tech Growth Fund' is visible in the portfolio dropdown, tab name, or portfolio manager list, indicating it has replaced 'My Portfolio'.

task_completion.description: Check if the transactions for AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA are still present in the portfolio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The stock tickers AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA are listed in the portfolio view with their respective units (100, 50, 25) under the 'Tech Growth Fund' portfolio.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the JStock GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the JStock graphical interface being used to rename the portfolio, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not edit the portfolio files directly using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are open showing XML or CSV portfolio files from the ~/.jstock directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the results are displayed within the actual JStock application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The portfolio name and transactions are shown inside the JStock application window, not in a terminal output or separate document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original portfolio name was 'My Portfolio'. The new portfolio name must be exactly 'Tech Growth Fund'. The portfolio must contain the following buy transactions: AAPL (100.0 units at 185.2), MSFT (50.0 units at 374.5), and NVDA (25.0 units at 615.3).
